steps.title: Establish the time frame
steps.content: The passage describes the Akkadian Empire, which "reigned in Mesopotamia from around 2334 BCE to 2154 BCE." The surrounding verbs are all past tense: "was vital," "reigned," "received." The blank describes what the Empire's people did during that same historical period.

steps.title: Choose the consistent tense
steps.content: Rule: verbs describing actions in the same time frame must use consistent tense. All context verbs are simple past, so the blank must also be simple past. A. "produce" — simple present. Fails: switches to present tense mid-narrative about events from 2334-2154 BCE. B. "produced" — simple past. Matches the historical time frame and the past-tense verbs throughout the passage. Correct. C. "will produce" — simple future. Fails: the empire no longer exists; future tense is impossible and inconsistent. D. "are producing" — present progressive. Fails: describes ongoing present action, but the empire ended over 4,000 years ago. That matches choice B — B is correct.
